surfaced_from: PWA chat 2026-05-25 15:49 UTC — John reported anyone can get to this chat if they know it exists
affects: A2A2H PWA / authentication and access control
capability_needed: Close PWA chat access-control bug so knowing the chat URL is not sufficient to read or use CTO chat
details: John reported a live security bug: anyone can get to the PWA chat if they know it exists. This is stronger than the earlier query-token hygiene finding in BACKLOG-009; it is an access-control failure for the human-facing CTO control room. The PWA must require real authenticated sessions, reject unauthenticated chat access, prevent URL-only/shareable-token access, and avoid leaking chat state to unauthorized viewers. Treat as P0 because the PWA is the control surface for John-to-CTO and may expose operational context.
search_trail: John explicitly reported the bug from live PWA use: 'Anyone can get to this chat if they know it exists.'
Reviewed existing BACKLOG-009, which covers URL query-token auth risk but does not fully capture John's live access-control failure report.
Earlier PWA audit found query-token auth and public HTTP/HTTPS exposure via Caddy to the PWA backend.

Prior clone handoff verification checked unauthenticated /api/messages rejection, but John's report shows the browser-level chat access model is still not acceptable and needs end-to-end access-control closure.
proposed_resolution: Replace URL/shareable-token access with a real login/session flow using HttpOnly Secure SameSite cookies or equivalent; require auth before serving chat UI and APIs; rotate/expire existing PWA tokens; redact URL/token logs; add regression tests for unauthenticated UI and API access; verify through a fresh browser with no prior state.
blast_radius: May temporarily log John out of the PWA and require a new bootstrap/login path. Could affect PWA background jobs, push subscriptions, and clone candidate PWA access if they assume token-in-URL behavior.
rollback_plan: Keep a short-lived localhost/operator bootstrap path during migration, verify John's device can log in, then disable the old URL-token path. If login breaks, restore the previous PWA service from git while keeping public access restricted at Caddy/firewall until fixed.
resolution_notes: Code repair committed: PWA chat shell and APIs now require signed HttpOnly Secure SameSite session cookies; API query-token auth is rejected; unauthenticated root serves a login form. Existing root/index ?token bootstrap is limited to migration and redirects to a clean URL. Live token rotation remains as follow-up after John confirms device access. Follow-up hotfix 2026-05-26 18:56 UTC removed the PWA shell from public GET routes and added query-token access-log redaction; verification artifact: logs/repairs/pwa-shell-access-control-hotfix-2026-05-26.md. 2026-05-26T21:32Z: live PWA auth was restored after a failed rotation path left current-token env var blank in /opt/cto/.env; unauthenticated root/API/query-token checks returned 401 and safe security gates passed. Token value was not recorded. 2026-05-26T21:56Z: PWA backend deployed dual-token rotation grace support: current-token env var signs new sessions, optional previous-token grace env var verifies previous login tokens and existing session cookies during rotation. Live token value was not changed or recorded; safe delivery/John confirmation remains pending.
resolution_log: 2026-05-26T21:25Z: current-token env var rotated by Claude Code per John's 'Rotate' directive. New bootstrap URL delivered in-conversation. See CTO-DECISION-018.

2026-05-26T21:32Z: Emergency rollback restored the previous non-empty runtime current-token env var from local secret backup after the attempted rotation left the live backend in no-auth dev mode. Rotation is no longer considered complete; it remains pending a safe delivery path and device confirmation.

2026-05-26T21:56Z: PWA backend deployed dual-token rotation grace support: current-token env var signs new sessions, optional previous-token grace env var verifies previous login tokens and existing session cookies during rotation. Live token value was not changed or recorded; safe delivery/John confirmation remains pending.
